title: intent-engineering finding schema
description: Contract returned by every ie-* lens sub-agent. Compact return carries merge-tier fields; full detail (why_it_matters, evidence) is written to the run-artifact JSON file.

title.description: One terse clause naming the surprise/violation. The scannable index, ~12 words.

severity.description: P0 critical surprise (data loss, security, broken contract). P1 high-impact, hit in normal use. P2 moderate. P3 minor.

confidence.description: Anchored. 100 verifiable from code/doc alone; 75 traceable and a real user/dev hits it; 50 depends on unseen context (advisory); <=25 speculative (suppress).

line.description: 1-based line. For a finding that spans a whole class/file (e.g. fat-model, callback-hell), use the declaration line (e.g. the `class` line) and set end_line.

end_line.description: Optional. Last line of a multi-line/file-spanning finding; omit for single-line findings.

fix_class.description: gated_auto: concrete reversible edit, caller may apply after judgment. manual: needs design input. advisory: report-only (learning, tradeoff note).

suggested_fix.description: Concrete action. Not 'consider' / 'might want to' — a specific change.

why_it_matters.description: Detail tier (artifact file). The expectation that was set vs the actual behavior — name the surprise.

evidence.description: Detail tier (artifact file). Quote/cite the code or doc line that grounds the finding.

tension.description: Optional. If this finding pits one principle against another (e.g. DWIM vs least-astonishment, YAGNI vs convention), name the tension instead of dogmatically picking a side.

smell.description: Architecture lens only. The structural smell id, e.g. fat-model, god-object, fat-controller, misused-service, callback-hell, query-in-view, law-of-demeter. See resources/frameworks/<stack>-architecture.md.

pattern.description: Architecture lens only. The design-pattern id involved (from resources/patterns/<stack>.yaml), or the literal 'unidentified' when a structural unit matches no known pattern. Used for classify / blocked / unknown findings.

observations.description: Soft notes that are real signal but not actionable findings (residual risks, things to watch). One string each.

scores.description: Audit/plan mode only. 0-10 per dimension this lens owns. Omit in review mode. Key names MUST be the canonical snake_case dimension ids in references/scoring-rubric.md for this lens — this schema enforces the value range and a snake_case key shape, but scoring-rubric.md is the authority for the exact key names the orchestrator assembles the posture table from.
